lecture_example: 1.1% threshold looks best after scanning all SPY history
control: Tune on train data only, then report untouched test data
why: Avoids selecting a parameter that only fit the past

lecture_example: Daily long/short flips make a high curve collapse after small costs
Each extra threshold/window can fit noise
control: Use few parameters and target at least 1000 rows per tuned price parameter
why: More degrees of freedom need more independent evidence

lecture_example: Live robots need holidays, early closes, errors, and broker API state
control: Maintain a going-live checklist separate from research logic
why: Production failures can occur even when the signal is correct
